model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the Beta project card
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (638,124) said dx=-113 dy=111 conf=0.80 -> (525,235)
  iter 1: at (525,235) said dx=-140 dy=40 conf=0.80 -> (385,275)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.reasoning_content:
The user wants me to click on the "Beta" project card.
The current crosshair position is (385, 275).
The "Beta" project card is the middle card in the top row.
Looking at the image:
- The "Beta" card center is approximately (385, 275). Wait, looking closer.
- The card spans from x ~ 270 to x ~ 500. Center x ~ 385.
- The card spans from y ~ 180 to y ~ 370. Center y ~ 275.
The crosshair is at (385, 275).
The crosshair is exactly on the "Beta" card. It's very close to the center.
Let's re-evaluate.
Crosshair is at (385, 275).
The "Beta" card:
Left edge: ~270
Right edge: ~500
Top edge: ~185
Bottom edge: ~375
Center: x = (270+500)/2 = 385, y = (185+375)/2 = 280.
The crosshair is at (385, 275). It's almost exactly in the center.
I'll report dx=0, dy=0 with high confidence.
